title: centaurea pseudoreflexa
intro: Centaurea pseudoreflexa, also known as False Cornflower, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Consuming Centaurea pseudoreflexa has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Centaurea pseudoreflexa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically to treat wounds and skin irritations.
